question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the ERC2612 permit path in buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral cause the bundler to receive maxBuyerAssets*(1-fee) but attempt to return maxBuyerAssets-filledBuyerAssets-referralFeeAssets to msg.sender, draining residual tokens from other users already held in MidnightBundles, violating the invariant that each caller's token balance is conserved across a bundler call, causing scoped impact: direct loss of other users' loan token funds held in MidnightBundles? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken, call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with maxBuyerAssets=1e18, filledBuyerAssets=1, referralFeePct=0, assert bundler balance after call is negative or drains a prior depositor.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the ERC2612 permit path in repayAndWithdrawCollateral cause Midnight.repay to credit units=assets-referralFeeAssets debt reduction while Midnight only receives units*(1-fee) tokens, inflating marketState.withdrawable beyond actual token balance, violating the solvency invariant that withdrawable <= actual loanToken balance held by Midnight, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where lenders cannot withdraw their full credit? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken, call repayAndWithdrawCollateral, assert Midnight.withdrawable(id) > loanToken.balanceOf(Midnight) after repay.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the ERC2612 permit path in supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget cause the bundler to receive assets*(1-fee) from msg.sender but call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, taker) supplying the full assets amount, causing Midnight to pull assets from the bundler while the bundler only holds assets*(1-fee), draining other users' collateral tokens held in MidnightBundles, violating the invariant that position.collateral[collateralIndex] matches actual tokens transferred, causing scoped impact: direct loss of other users' collateral tokens? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer collateralToken, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with collateralSupplies[0].assets=1e18, assert bundler balance goes negative or drains prior depositor.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit (try/catch)] Can an attacker front-run a victim's ERC2612 permit call by submitting the same (owner, spender, value, deadline, v, r, s) to the token contract directly, consuming the nonce, causing the try/catch in pullToken to silently swallow the PermitError, then safeTransferFrom proceeds using the victim's pre-existing allowance which may be a different amount than intended, causing the bundler to pull a different amount than the permit was for, violating the invariant that the pulled amount equals the permit-authorized amount, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit or debt accounting when filledBuyerAssets or repaid units are computed from a wrong pulled amount? Proof idea: front-run permit in a test, assert safeTransferFrom uses stale allowance and bundler accounting diverges from user intent.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker using a DAI-style permit token (permit(address,address,uint256,uint256,bool,uint8,bytes32,bytes32) instead of ERC2612) under PermitKind.ERC2612 cause the try/catch to silently swallow the ABI-mismatch revert, then safeTransferFrom proceeds using the taker's pre-existing allowance, potentially pulling a different amount than maxBuyerAssets if the pre-existing allowance is smaller, causing the bundler to have insufficient funds for the take loop, violating the invariant that filledBuyerAssets <= actual tokens available in bundler, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit accounting or unexpected revert mid-take leaving partial state? Proof idea: deploy DAI-style permit token, set PermitKind.ERC2612, assert safeTransferFrom uses stale allowance and take loop behavior diverges.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker using an ERC777-compatible loan token that triggers a tokensToSend hook during the permit call (before safeTransferFrom) reenter MidnightBundles or Midnight during the permit execution, observing a state where forceApproveMax has not yet been called and MIDNIGHT's allowance is 0, causing the reentrant take to fail or use stale accounting, violating the invariant that credit/debt state is consistent before and after the permit+transfer sequence, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit or debt accounting from partial state reuse? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 loanToken with tokensToSend hook that reenters buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral, assert credit state is consistent.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.forceApproveMax / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged attacker exploit the forceApproveMax skip condition (allowance >= type(uint96).max/2) combined with a fee-on-transfer loan token to leave residual tokens in MidnightBundles from a previous call, then a subsequent caller's pullToken+forceApproveMax sequence causes MIDNIGHT to pull those residual tokens as part of the new caller's take, crediting the new caller with units backed by the previous caller's tokens, violating the invariant that each user's credit increase corresponds to their own token payment, causing scoped impact: direct loss of prior caller's residual loan tokens? Proof idea: run two sequential buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral calls with fee-on-transfer token, assert residual from call 1 is consumed in call 2.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker pass a TokenPermit with PermitKind.ERC2612 where the permit.data encodes a deadline already expired, causing the permit to silently fail via try/catch, then safeTransferFrom uses the taker's existing allowance which may be type(uint256).max from a prior approval, allowing the bundler to pull maxBuyerAssets even though the user intended to revoke access via permit expiry, violating the invariant that permit expiry prevents unauthorized token pulls, causing scoped impact: unauthorized loan token transfer leading to incorrect credit accounting? Proof idea: set permit deadline = block.timestamp - 1, assert safeTransferFrom still succeeds using stale max allowance.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged operator (msg.sender != onBehalf) call repayAndWithdrawCollateral where the ERC2612 permit is signed by msg.sender for MidnightBundles as spender, but the repay credits onBehalf's debt reduction, causing a scenario where msg.sender's tokens are used to repay onBehalf's debt and onBehalf then withdraws collateral, violating the invariant that token payment and debt/collateral accounting are attributed to the same account, causing scoped impact: msg.sender loses loan tokens while onBehalf gains collateral without paying? Proof idea: call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with onBehalf != msg.sender, assert msg.sender's token balance decreases while onBehalf's collateral increases.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker craft a TokenPermit.data that ABI-decodes to a valid (deadline, v, r, s) tuple but where the encoded bytes also contain extra trailing data, causing IERC20Permit.permit to be called with correct parameters but the abi.decode in pullToken to succeed with garbage v/r/s values if the token's permit implementation is lenient, causing the permit to set a wrong allowance, then safeTransferFrom pulls the wrong amount, violating the invariant that pulled amount equals the intended maxBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit accounting from wrong token pull amount? Proof idea: fuzz permit.data encoding with extra bytes, assert pulled amount matches maxBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token cause the bundler to receive filledSellerAssets*(1-fee) from MIDNIGHT's safeTransfer but then attempt to send referralFeeAssets + targetSellerAssets = targetFilledSellerAssets to referralFeeRecipient and receiver, where targetFilledSellerAssets > bundler's actual balance, draining other users' tokens from the bundler, violating the invariant that total outflows from bundler equal total inflows per call, causing scoped impact: direct loss of other users' loan tokens held in MidnightBundles? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget, assert bundler balance conservation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token whose permit implementation accepts any signature (e.g., ecrecover returns address(0) and the token does not check for zero address) to call pullToken with a garbage signature, causing permit to succeed and set allowance to amount, then safeTransferFrom pulls amount from the victim's address (from=msg.sender) without the victim having signed anything, violating the invariant that token transfers require valid authorization, causing scoped impact: unauthorized loan token transfer leading to incorrect credit accounting? Proof idea: deploy token with permit that skips zero-address check, call pullToken with zero v/r/s, assert transfer succeeds without valid signature.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the referral fee calculation referralFeeAssets = targetBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) combined with a fee-on-transfer loan token where the bundler receives targetBuyerAssets*(1-fee) but the referral fee is computed on the full targetBuyerAssets, causing the bundler to send referralFeeAssets to referralFeeRecipient from tokens it does not fully hold, draining other users' tokens, violating the invariant that referral fee is only taken from tokens actually received, causing scoped impact: direct loss of other users' loan tokens? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct and fee-on-transfer rate, assert bundler balance >= 0 after call.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker pass PermitKind.ERC2612 with a permit.data that encodes a value (amount in permit) different from the amount parameter passed to pullToken, causing IERC20Permit.permit to set allowance to the encoded value while safeTransferFrom transfers the amount parameter, creating a mismatch where the allowance set by permit does not cover the safeTransferFrom amount, causing safeTransferFrom to revert or use a stale allowance, violating the invariant that permit authorization covers exactly the transferred amount, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit accounting from wrong transfer amount? Proof idea: encode permit.data with value != amount, assert safeTransferFrom behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker supply collateral via ERC2612 permit where the permit is for a collateral token that rebases (balance increases after transfer), causing the bundler to receive more collateral than collateralSupplies[i].assets, then supplyCollateral credits exactly assets to position.collateral[collateralIndex], leaving excess collateral tokens stranded in the bundler, which are then consumed by a subsequent caller's forceApproveMax+supplyCollateral, violating the invariant that collateral accounting matches actual tokens transferred, causing scoped impact: incorrect collateral accounting and loss of excess tokens? Proof idea: deploy rebasing collateral token, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget, assert bundler balance and position.collateral conservation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token whose permit function has a non-standard signature that matches the IERC20Permit interface bytes but internally sets allowance to type(uint256).max regardless of the value parameter, causing safeTransferFrom to pull only amount but leaving a type(uint256).max allowance on the bundler for MIDNIGHT, allowing a subsequent attacker to call forceApproveMax (which skips reset since allowance >= type(uint96).max/2) and then drain the bundler's entire token balance via a crafted take, violating the invariant that bundler allowances are bounded by user-authorized amounts, causing scoped impact: direct loss of all loan tokens held in MidnightBundles? Proof idea: deploy token with permit that sets max allowance, assert forceApproveMax skip condition and subsequent drain.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged operator call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct close to WAD-1, causing units = assets - referralFeeAssets to be nearly 0, so the repay reduces onBehalf's debt by nearly 0 units while the bundler pulls the full assets from msg.sender and sends nearly all to referralFeeRecipient, violating the invariant that token payment corresponds to debt reduction, causing scoped impact: msg.sender loses assets while onBehalf's debt is barely reduced and collateral is withdrawn? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD, assert units repaid proportional to assets paid.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token that emits a Transfer event during permit (some ERC777-compatible tokens) triggering a tokensReceived hook on the bundler contract if the bundler implements ERC777TokensRecipient, causing reentrancy into pullToken before safeTransferFrom is called, allowing the reentrant call to observe the state where forceApproveMax has already been called but no tokens have been transferred yet, enabling a double-spend of the MIDNIGHT allowance, violating the invariant that credit increases correspond to actual token inflows, causing scoped impact: credit minted without corresponding token payment? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 token with tokensReceived hook, assert credit vs token balance invariant.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker pass a TokenPermit with PermitKind.None (no permit) while having pre-approved the bundler for exactly maxBuyerAssets, then a front-runner calls the same bundler function with PermitKind.ERC2612 and a valid permit for the same token and amount, causing the front-runner's permit to consume the victim's nonce, then the victim's safeTransferFrom uses the pre-existing allowance, but the front-runner's take has already filled the offers, causing the victim's take loop to find no consumable units and revert with OutOfOffers after the victim's tokens have been pulled, violating the invariant that token pull and credit gain are atomic, causing scoped impact: victim's tokens are pulled but no credit is received? Proof idea: simulate front-run scenario, assert victim's token balance and credit state.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token whose permit function reverts with a non-standard error (not a standard revert) that causes the try/catch to not catch it (e.g., if the token uses assembly revert with no data and the Solidity try/catch only catches errors with selectors), causing the entire pullToken call to revert rather than silently continuing, violating the invariant that permit failure is always tolerated and safeTransferFrom always proceeds, causing scoped impact: unexpected revert mid-bundle leaving partial collateral supply state in Midnight? Proof idea: deploy token with assembly-level revert in permit, assert try/catch behavior in Solidity 0.8.34.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the asymmetric referral fee formula (sell side: referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD-referralFeePct) vs buy side: mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD)) combined with a permit for exactly targetSellerAssets+referralFeeAssets to cause a rounding discrepancy where filledSellerAssets != targetFilledSellerAssets by 1 wei due to mulDivDown, causing the require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets) to revert after collateral has already been supplied to Midnight, violating the invariant that collateral supply and sell are atomic, causing scoped impact: collateral locked in Midnight with no corresponding debt reduction? Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets and referralFeePct near rounding boundaries, assert atomicity.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token that implements permit by calling transferFrom internally (some non-standard implementations), causing the permit call to both set the allowance AND transfer tokens, then safeTransferFrom transfers again, resulting in a double transfer of amount tokens from the user, violating the invariant that pullToken transfers exactly amount tokens from the user, causing scoped impact: user loses 2*amount tokens while only amount is credited to the bundler for the take? Proof idea: deploy token with permit that internally calls transferFrom, assert user balance decrease equals amount not 2*amount.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token with 6 decimals as the loan token where the permit value is encoded as a 6-decimal amount but the bundler's take loop computes filledBuyerAssets in 18-decimal units, causing a decimal mismatch where the permit authorizes a 6-decimal amount but the take expects an 18-decimal amount, violating the invariant that permit value equals the token amount transferred equals the amount used in take accounting, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit accounting due to decimal mismatch between permit value and take units? Proof idea: deploy 6-decimal loanToken, fuzz permit value vs take units, assert credit accounting consistency.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral where the take loop partially fills (filledUnits < targetUnits) and reverts with OutOfOffers after some takes have already updated consumed[maker][group] in Midnight, but the permit-authorized tokens have already been pulled from the user, violating the invariant that token pull and offer consumption are atomic, causing scoped impact: user's tokens are pulled and offers are partially consumed but no credit is received? Proof idea: set up insufficient offers, call with ERC2612 permit, assert user token balance and credit state after revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IERC20Permit.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: MidnightBundles.pullToken / IERC20Permit.permit] Can an unprivileged taker use a token whose permit function has a reentrancy vulnerability that allows the token contract to call back into MidnightBundles.pullToken during the permit execution, causing a second pullToken call to complete (pulling tokens and calling forceApproveMax) before the first pullToken's safeTransferFrom executes, resulting in MIDNIGHT having a max allowance from the bundler but the bundler's balance being double-counted, violating the invariant that forceApproveMax is called exactly once per pullToken invocation, causing scoped impact: incorrect credit accounting from double allowance setup? Proof idea: deploy reentrant permit token, assert bundler balance and MIDNIGHT allowance after nested pullToken.
